scope.summary:
Goal: Create a reusable, keyboard-accessible context-menu infrastructure module that all other C5 MTs will import. No native OS browser/webview menu is available in a pure Rust egui app — the context menu must be a first-class egui popup painted by the app itself.

Expected diff shape: Create ONE new source file `src/frontend/context_menu/mod.rs` (and register it in `src/frontend/mod.rs` or the crate root). Optionally split into `src/frontend/context_menu/types.rs` + `src/frontend/context_menu/widget.rs` if lines exceed ~300, but the public API surface must live in `src/frontend/context_menu/mod.rs`.

Files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/context_menu/mod.rs` — the entire infra lives here.
- EDIT `src/frontend/mod.rs` (or `src/frontend/lib.rs`) to add `pub mod context_menu;`.

Public API to expose (all types and functions must be `pub`):

```rust
// A single menu item. `id` must be globally stable per surface type + action.
pub struct ContextMenuItem {
    pub id: &'static str,          // stable author_id used for AccessKit node
    pub label: &'static str,
    pub shortcut_hint: Option<&'static str>,
    pub enabled: bool,
    pub kind: MenuItemKind,
}

pub enum MenuItemKind {
    Action,             // fires immediately on confirm
    Submenu(Vec<ContextMenuItem>),  // opens a nested popup
    Separator,          // visual divider, not interactive
}

// Open request: caller sets anchor point (screen pos) and the item list.
pub struct ContextMenuRequest {
    pub anchor: egui::Pos2,
    pub items: Vec<ContextMenuItem>,
}

// State stored in the egui Context's memory (keyed by a stable TypeId).
pub struct ContextMenuState {
    pub open: bool,
    pub request: Option<ContextMenuRequest>,
    pub active_submenu: Option<usize>,  // index of the item whose submenu is open
    pub keyboard_cursor: Option<usize>, // keyboard-navigated item index
}
impl Default for ContextMenuState { ... }

// Call site: show context menu if one is requested. Returns the id of the
// activated item, or None.
pub fn show_context_menu(
    ctx: &egui::Context,
    ui: &mut egui::Ui,
    state: &mut ContextMenuState,
) -> Option<&'static str>;

// Trigger: call on secondary-click (right-click) or Shift+F10 / the Menu key.
pub fn request_context_menu(ctx: &egui::Context, anchor: egui::Pos2, items: Vec<ContextMenuItem>);

// Dismiss the open menu without firing an action.
pub fn dismiss_context_menu(ctx: &egui::Context);
```

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Add the `context_menu` module to `src/frontend/mod.rs`.
2. In `mod.rs`, define `MenuItemKind`, `ContextMenuItem`, `ContextMenuRequest`, `ContextMenuState` structs/enums.
3. Store `ContextMenuState` in `egui::Context::memory_mut` using a unique `Id` derived from `egui::Id::new("hs_context_menu_global")`. This ensures only ONE context menu is open at a time across the whole app (standard desktop behavior).
4. `request_context_menu`: mutates the stored state to set `open=true`, saves the `ContextMenuRequest`.
5. `dismiss_context_menu`: sets `open=false`, clears `request`.
6. `show_context_menu`: must be called once per frame from the root-level egui paint pass (not inside a pane, so the popup can overflow pane boundaries). Implementation:
   a. Read `ContextMenuState` from memory. If `!state.open` or `state.request.is_none()`, return `None`.
   b. Create an `egui::Area` with `order(egui::Order::Foreground)` anchored at `request.anchor`, constrained to stay inside the screen rect (clamp if near edge).
   c. Inside the Area, show an `egui::Frame` with a background fill, a thin border, and a drop shadow (use the theme's `ContextMenu` fill; fall back to `egui::Visuals::window_fill`).
   d. Iterate items: separators use `ui.separator()`; regular items use `ui.add_enabled(item.enabled, egui::Button::new(item.label).shortcut_text(item.shortcut_hint.unwrap_or("")))`; submenu items show a trailing chevron `">"` suffix.
   e. Keyboard navigation: on each frame, read `egui::Key::ArrowDown`/`ArrowUp` to move `keyboard_cursor`; `Enter` or `Space` activates the cursor item; `Escape` dismisses; `ArrowRight` opens a submenu.
   f. Submenus: when a submenu item is hovered or keyboard-activated, open a SECOND `egui::Area` offset to the right of the first, rendering the sub-items using the same pattern recursively (only one level of recursion is required for V1; deeper nesting is not needed).
   g. Click-outside dismissal: if the user clicks outside all open menu Areas in the current frame, call `dismiss_context_menu`.
   h. Return the `id` of the activated item if one was clicked or Enter-confirmed; otherwise `None`.
7. AccessKit obligations: every rendered menu item button must have its `egui::Response` extended with an AccessKit `author_id` of the form `format!("ctx_menu_item_{}", item.id)`. Use `egui::Response::on_hover_text` to expose the label for accessibility, and mark enabled/disabled state via `egui::Button::sense(Sense::click())` vs. none. In V1, the AccessKit node for each item is role `MenuItem`, name = `item.label`, action = `Default` (click). This is set by calling the AccessKit node-building helper established in MT-025 (`set_accesskit_author_id`) if it exists; otherwise add a TODO comment referencing MT-025.
8. There is NO React equivalent of this component — the React app has no context menu system at all. This is a net-new Handshake-native capability. The closest analog for behavior reference is VS Code's context menu (keyboard navigation, submenu nesting, click-outside dismiss) and the egui `popup_below_widget` pattern.
9. No backend API call in this MT — the infra is pure UI state. Backend calls happen when an action is confirmed and routed by MT-020 / MT-021.

Dependencies/unblock conditions:
- MT-005 (Pane registry) must exist so the root paint pass is known.
- MT-006 or MT-007 (split/tab system) establishes the egui render loop structure this MT hooks into.
- MT-025 (AccessKit author_id helper) is referenced but not required before merge — add TODO if MT-025 not yet merged.

Risk if missed: MT-020 and MT-021 have no shared primitive to build on and would each reinvent divergent popup logic, making them inconsistent and impossible to style/test centrally.
acceptance_criteria: File `src/frontend/context_menu/mod.rs` exists and is registered in the crate module tree (`pub mod context_menu` in `src/frontend/mod.rs`).
`cargo check` (or `cargo build`) on the frontend crate passes with no errors introduced by this MT.
`show_context_menu`, `request_context_menu`, and `dismiss_context_menu` are all `pub` and importable from sibling modules.
`ContextMenuItem`, `ContextMenuRequest`, `ContextMenuState`, and `MenuItemKind` are all `pub` with the field/variant layout defined in the implementation notes.

A unit test in the same file (or `src/frontend/context_menu/tests.rs`) proves that calling `request_context_menu` followed by `dismiss_context_menu` leaves `ContextMenuState.open == false` and `request == None` (can use egui's headless context via `egui::Context::default()`).
Keyboard navigation: arrow keys move cursor, Escape dismisses, Enter confirms — proven by an egui_kittest harness test OR a unit test that simulates key events via `egui::RawInput`.
A submenu item with `MenuItemKind::Submenu(vec![...])` renders a child Area when hovered (proven by egui_kittest or a screenshot test that shows the child popup).
Click-outside a menu dismisses it within one frame (egui_kittest or unit test verifying state after synthetic pointer press outside the menu rect).
Every menu item button has an AccessKit `author_id` of the form `ctx_menu_item_{item.id}` set on the egui Response (or a `// TODO MT-025` comment if the MT-025 helper is not yet merged).
No native OS/browser context menu APIs are used; the entire system is egui-drawn.
The module compiles and the test suite passes: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend context_menu`.
proof_targets: Run `cargo test -p handshake_frontend context_menu -- --nocapture` and paste the passing output. All tests green.
Run `cargo check` or `cargo build --workspace` (excluding backend targets if needed) and show zero new errors.
If egui_kittest is available: run a harness test that opens a menu, presses ArrowDown twice, presses Enter, and asserts the returned item id matches the third item. Paste the passing test output.

Screenshot (egui_kittest rendered frame or manual run) showing: a context menu popup with at least 3 items, separator visible, keyboard cursor highlighted on an item, and no native OS context menu UI.
implementation_notes: Use `egui::Area::new(egui::Id::new("hs_ctx_menu_root")).order(egui::Order::Foreground)` for the main menu popup.
Screen-edge clamping: after determining the Area rect, if it extends past `ctx.screen_rect().right()`, shift left; if past `.bottom()`, shift up. This prevents menus from opening off-screen.
Submenu Area id: `egui::Id::new("hs_ctx_menu_sub_{parent_item_id}")` to avoid id collisions.
For the click-outside check: collect the combined bounding rect of all open menu Areas; if `ctx.input(|i| i.pointer.any_click())` is true and the click position is outside that rect, dismiss.
Theme integration: use `ui.visuals().window_fill` for background, `ui.visuals().window_stroke` for border. When the dark/light theme system (MT-003) is available, it will automatically apply.
Separator: `ui.separator()` with a small vertical margin.

Shortcut hint: render as a right-aligned dimmed label inside the item row using `ui.with_layout(egui::Layout::right_to_left(egui::Align::Center), |ui| { ui.label(hint); })` BEFORE the item button, so the layout order puts the hint on the right.
Do NOT use `egui::popup_below_widget` — that ties the popup to a widget rect and does not support absolute anchor positioning needed for right-click.
Avoid `egui::modal` — context menus must not be modal; the user can click outside to dismiss.
V1 scope: only ONE context menu + ONE level of submenu open at a time. Do not implement cascading multi-level submenus.
ports_from_react: NONE — no context-menu system exists in the React app (app/src/**). This is a net-new Handshake-native primitive. Behavioral reference only: VS Code context menu (keyboard nav, submenu, click-outside), not React source.
binds_backend_api: NONE in this MT — the infra is pure UI state. Backend calls are made by callers in MT-020 and MT-021 when a menu item action is activated.

risks: Menu popup renders outside the egui clip rect of its parent pane and gets clipped/invisible if not using Foreground order and a top-level Area.

Multiple callers simultaneously call `request_context_menu` in the same frame, causing last-write-wins to silently drop one menu open request — user right-clicks in two panes concurrently (swarm agents) and only one menu appears.
Keyboard events consumed by the context menu bleed into the underlying focused widget (e.g. ArrowDown in a list also moves the list selection).
Screen-edge clamping is skipped, menus open partially off-screen on small monitors or near taskbar.

AccessKit node ids for menu items collide if two different menus share the same item.id string (both menus open at once — impossible in V1 but becomes a risk if the global-single constraint is later relaxed).
Submenu opens but is itself clipped by the main menu Area rect if not placed in a separate top-level Area.
Click-outside detection misses the frame the menu opened (first-frame false-positive dismiss).
The module is not registered in `src/frontend/mod.rs`, so MT-020/MT-021 cannot import it (`use crate::context_menu::...` fails at compile time).
minimum_controls: Use `egui::Order::Foreground` for ALL menu Areas — this is required to paint above all other content and bypass pane clipping.
Enforce a single-open invariant: `request_context_menu` always replaces any existing open request. Document this constraint in the module-level doc comment.
Consume key events with `ctx.input_mut(|i| i.consume_key(...))` so ArrowDown/Up/Enter/Escape are not forwarded to underlying widgets.
Apply screen-edge clamping as described in implementation_notes — covered by the acceptance criterion requiring a screenshot showing the menu is not clipped.
Use per-item stable `id: &'static str` in `ContextMenuItem` — callers MUST use namespaced ids (e.g. `tab.close`, `editor.cut`) documented in MT-020/MT-021.
Place submenu in a separate `egui::Area` (not inside the parent Area) to avoid clipping.
Guard click-outside dismiss with a one-frame grace: skip the dismiss check on the very frame the menu was opened (track `opened_this_frame: bool` in `ContextMenuState`).
Add a compile-time test (`cargo test`) that panics if `context_menu` is not reachable as `crate::context_menu` from the frontend crate root.

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms PASS: the reusable egui-Popup-drawn context-menu primitive honestly delivers the MT-019 intent (typed item model, builder, secondary-click open, wrapping keyboard nav with real AccessKit focus cursor, one-level submenu, Escape/click-away dismiss, stable ctx-menu.{id} MenuItem author_ids) all proven LIVE through egui_kittest reading the real AccessKit tree and real key_press dispatch — no stubs, no #[ignore]'d required behavior, no mock-only proof; the API-name divergence from the literal contract is a disclosed, downstream-consumed (MT-020 context_menu_surfaces), architecturally superior egui::Popup choice, not a code failure under the GOOD-CODE-vs-INTENT bar.
code_examined: D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake Worktrees/wtc-worksurface-shell-v1/src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs (name_node / accesskit_node_builder reference pattern lines 360-362, 528-534)
git show --stat dc6e1457 (MT-019 origin commit: context_menu.rs + lib.rs + test_context_menu.rs, no build config)

acceptance_summary.criterion: context_menu module exists and is registered (pub mod context_menu in crate tree)
acceptance_summary.evidence: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/context_menu.rs (663 lines) present; lib.rs:12 `pub mod context_menu;`. Contract said src/frontend/context_menu/mod.rs + src/frontend/mod.rs, but the real native crate layout is src/frontend/handshake_native/src/context_menu.rs registered in that crate's lib.rs — same registration intent, correct crate location. Compile-time reachability is implicitly proven because tests/test_context_menu.rs imports `handshake_native::context_menu::{ContextMenu, ContextMenuItem}` and compiles.

acceptance_summary.criterion: cargo check/build passes with no new errors
acceptance_summary.evidence: Validator ground-truth (relied on per directive): cargo build --tests exit 0 for the whole handshake_native crate; this file + its test binary are part of it.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Open / dismiss / is-open helpers are pub and importable
acceptance_summary.evidence: context_menu.rs:472 `pub fn request_open`, :484 `pub fn dismiss`, :489 `pub fn is_open`, :236 `pub fn show_on`. Names differ from contract's show_context_menu/request_context_menu/dismiss_context_menu but the open/render/dismiss/is-open behavior is fully present, pub, and consumed by context_menu_surfaces (MT-020, grep-confirmed).

acceptance_summary.criterion: ContextMenuItem / kind / model types are pub with the intended field/variant layout
acceptance_summary.evidence: context_menu.rs:79 `pub enum MenuItemKind { Action, Submenu(Vec<ContextMenuItem>), Separator }`; :98 `pub struct ContextMenuItem { id:&'static str, label, shortcut_hint:Option<&'static str>, enabled:bool, disabled_reason, kind }`. Contract's ContextMenuRequest/ContextMenuState replaced by egui Popup memory + a private MenuNavState — the contract's own implementation_notes explicitly say to store state in egui Context memory, so state-in-egui-memory is the contract's stated intent. Unit test model_carries_typed_items (:518) asserts the layout.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Unit test proves request->dismiss leaves the menu closed
acceptance_summary.evidence: tests::request_open_then_dismiss_round_trips (context_menu.rs:570) drives a real headless egui::Context through two ctx.run frames: after request_open is_open==true AND egui::Popup::position_of_id stores the anchor; after dismiss is_open==false. Non-tautological: reads real egui Popup memory, not a hand-set bool.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Keyboard nav: arrows move cursor, Escape dismisses, Enter confirms (kittest or RawInput)
acceptance_summary.evidence: test_context_menu.rs LIVE kittest: arrow_down_twice_then_enter_dispatches_third_leaf (3-leaf menu, ArrowDown x2 + Enter -> 'triple.three' as one gesture, proof_target #3 literal), arrow_down_then_enter_confirms_third_actionable_item, arrow_up_wraps_to_last_actionable_item (reads live AccessKit is_focused()), escape_closes_menu_without_action — all real key_press events driving real render_menu_root nav + real dispatch. Plus unit tests step_highlight_skips_and_wraps / first_navigable_picks_first_actionable for the pure logic.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Submenu item renders a child Area when opened
acceptance_summary.evidence: render_items (context_menu.rs:381) uses ui.menu_button for Submenu kind (separate Foreground Area, not clipped — red-team R5/R6); kittest submenu_opens_and_child_dispatches opens 'Move to' and dispatches child 'tab.move-to.pane-b' read from the live tree.

egui Popup provides click-outside + one-frame grace (was_open_last_frame, doc'd at context_menu.rs:44-45); kittest click_away_closes_menu opens then primary-clicks the surface outside the popup and asserts all ctx-menu.* nodes gone.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Every menu item button has AccessKit author_id ctx-menu.{id} (or TODO MT-025)
acceptance_summary.evidence: name_menu_node (context_menu.rs:438) calls the genuine egui ctx.accesskit_node_builder setting Role::MenuItem + set_author_id(`ctx-menu.{id}`) + label — byte-for-byte mirroring top_menu_bar::name_node:528-534 (verified). kittest open_menu_items_are_all_named + secondary_click_opens_menu_with_named_items read the LIVE AccessKit tree and assert every MenuItem node is named. Contract allowed a // TODO MT-025 fallback; instead the real established pattern is used directly — strictly stronger than the contract minimum.

acceptance_summary.criterion: No native OS/browser context menu APIs; entire system egui-drawn
acceptance_summary.evidence: Built on egui::Popup::context_menu + egui::Button/menu_button; module doc (context_menu.rs:29-30) explicitly states no native/OS/webview menu. No webview/OS-menu calls present.

Validator ground-truth: curated suite green incl. all 29 integration binaries; test_context_menu.rs has 11 tests, NONE marked #[ignore] (verified by reading the whole file), and the in-file unit tests mod (7 tests) compiles + runs. 0 failures.

spec_realism.detail: Sub-rule 1: independent grep of context_menu.rs for todo!()/unimplemented!()/unreachable!()/'not yet wired'/'deferred to'/'pending MT-'/'Unavailable' on the required path = NO MATCHES. The only panic!() in the module is a legitimate test-assertion branch (tests::model_carries_typed_items expected-submenu else-arm). No early stub returns on the open/render/dispatch path. Sub-rule 2: the MT claims a Handshake-owned AccessKit-tree resource (Role::MenuItem nodes with stable author_ids for out-of-process steering). Proof is REAL not mock-only: name_menu_node uses the genuine egui::Context::accesskit_node_builder API (confirmed identical to the already-shipped top_menu_bar::name_node), and egui_kittest tests read the ACTUAL produced AccessKit tree via harness.root().children_recursive().accesskit_node().author_id()/role()/is_focused(). Keyboard nav is proven by real harness.key_press events driving the real render_menu_root highlight + FAKE_PRIMARY_CLICKED dispatch — not a state poke. No #[ignore] hides required behavior. Sub-rule 3 (implementer self-cert): waived per operator directive — THIS independent review is the certification; lifecycle.status PENDING is paperwork, not a code FAIL.

concerns_non_blocking: API-surface deviation from the literal contract (NOT a blocker under the GOOD-CODE-vs-INTENT bar): the contract named show_context_menu/request_context_menu/dismiss_context_menu/ContextMenuState/ContextMenuRequest; the implementation exposes a ContextMenu builder + show_on/render_into and request_open/dismiss/is_open, with state in egui Popup memory + a private MenuNavState. Independently confirmed the deviation is (a) openly disclosed in the module doc with rationale, (b) consumed by the real downstream MT-020 context_menu_surfaces.rs (grep-confirmed: ContextMenu::new/ContextMenuItem/show_on all used), so it is a coherent live API, not dead code. The contract's own implementation_notes already mandated storing state in egui Context memory, so 'state-in-egui-memory' matches contract intent; only the public symbol names diverge.

Red-team R3 (arrow-key bleed): render_menu_root consume_key's the arrows but the doc honestly states egui's own directional focus already fired in begin_pass before the closure runs, landing harmlessly on the same menu-focusable items. consume_key still stops the arrow events leaking to other widgets in-frame. There is no dedicated test asserting an UNDERLYING list/widget does NOT also move on ArrowDown while the menu is open. Honest V1 tradeoff, candidate isolation test for a later MT — not a bug.

proof_target #4 (pixel screenshot of the rendered popup: separator + keyboard-cursor highlight) was explicitly punted to MT-029 per the commit message; the kittest AccessKit-tree + is_focused() assertions substitute for V1. Acceptable; the pixel-level visual proof remains owed by MT-029.

Paperwork only (per operator directive, NOT a code FAIL): MT-019.json lifecycle.status is still PENDING and validator_verdict PENDING (folded-build self-cert). This independent review is the certification.

This MT touched no build config (git show --stat confirms only context_menu.rs + lib.rs + test_context_menu.rs), so the Handshake_Artifacts-in-tree artifact-hygiene concern does not originate here, and this MT does not touch state-recovery / layout-persistence (pure egui-memory popup state).
